hook.text: A company has $5 billion in obligations that appear nowhere on its balance sheet.

buildup.text: Operating leases, special-purpose entities, and guarantees can be kept off the main statements.

discovery.text: These items still represent real obligations—they just aren't visible at first glance.

twist.text: Enron hid billions in debt through off-balance-sheet entities. It fooled everyone for years.

climax.text: Post-Enron rules now force many off-balance-sheet items onto the books—but gaps remain.

punchline.text: What's not on the balance sheet can still sink the ship.

quiz.question: Why are off-balance-sheet items dangerous?
options: They hide real obligations from investors
